steps.title: Recognize the exponential form
steps.content: The function $f(t) = 34,000(2)^t$ is in the standard form $f(t) = a(b)^t$, where $a = 34,000$ is the coefficient and $b = 2$ is the base. In any exponential function of this form, the coefficient $a$ represents the initial value—the function's output when $t = 0$.

steps.title: Evaluate at the initial time
steps.content:
Substituting $t = 0$:
$$f(0) = 34,000(2)^0 = 34,000(1) = 34,000$$
Since $t$ represents hours after the initial measurement, $t = 0$ is the initial measurement moment itself.

Therefore, 34,000 is the number of bacteria in the population at that moment.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
